title: westringia parvifolia
intro: Westringia parvifolia, also known as the coastal rosemary, is a shrub native to Australia and a member of the mint family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral activities, which can be used to treat various infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of Westringia parvifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Westringia parvifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and health.
